next_steps: Immunohistochemical staining for CD1a, Langerin, CD68, and CD163 to characterize histiocytic phenotype
BRAF V600E mutation analysis of biopsy tissue
Consultation with a hematopathology expert specializing in histiocytic disorders
Evaluate for systemic involvement via cardiac MRI and long bone skeletal survey
Comprehensive metabolic and inflammatory marker panel including C-reactive protein and serum protein electrophoresis

differential_diagnoses: IgG4-related disease with secondary bone involvement
next_steps: Perform immunohistochemical staining on the tibial biopsy specimen for CD68, CD163, CD1a, S100, and Factor XIIIa to confirm histiocytic lineage and subtype
Test for BRAF V600E mutation in bone lesion tissue via droplet digital PCR or NGS
Measure serum IgG4, total IgG, and soluble interleukin-2 receptor (sIL-2R) levels
Obtain cardiac MRI to screen for classic 'coated aorta' or pericardial/myocardial infiltration characteristic of Erdheim-Chester disease
Perform a whole-body skeletal survey/CT to map the extent of osteosclerotic and lytic bone lesions
Refer the patient to a multidisciplinary center of excellence for histiocytic disorders for specialized pathology review and management planning
